statement.en: Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Coupled Demand Forecasting and Supply Chain Optimization
statement.summary: Supply chain resilience and efficiency are vital in industries characterized by volatile demand and uncertain supply, such as textiles and personal protective equipment (PPE). Traditional forecasting and optimization approaches often operate in isolation, limiting their real-world effectiveness. This paper proposes a Hybrid AI Framework for Demand-Supply Forecasting and Optimization (HAF-DS), which integrates a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based demand forecasting module with a mixed integer linear programming (MILP) optimization layer. The LSTM captures temporal and contextual demand dependencies, while the optimization layer prescribes cost-efficient replenishment and allocation decisions. The framework jointly minimizes forecasting error and operational cost through embedding-based fea

proposer: Nusrat Yasmin Nadia, Md Habibul Arif, Habibor Rahman Rabby et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes a hybrid methodology (LSTM + MILP) for supply chain optimization. The approach is technically sound in principle but classified as FLOWING because: (1) abstract is incomplete ('embedding-based fea...' truncated), (2) publication date is 2026-04-24 (future date, suggests preprint/provisional status), (3) empirical validation results not provided in abstract, (4) real-world effectiveness claims require field testing not evident from abstract alone. The core proposition is theoretically valid but validation status indeterminate.
